source.paper: A Unified Newton Barrier Method for Multibody Dynamics
source.phase: Phase 7: Private GPU Parity And Speed

policy.row_gate: Measured rows require same-scene CPU/GPU parity, explicit tolerance, setup/transfer/readback/kernel/solve timing, and speedup against the DART CPU path.
policy.public_api: No public CUDA, stream, device, memory-pool, backend-task, solver-registry, or package-surface exposure is allowed by this packet.
policy.ordering: Promote private GPU evidence in the roadmap order: stencils/candidates, CCD/line search, local barrier/friction kernels, PSD projection, assembly/linear solve, then scene-level parity only after the private packet rows are stable.
policy.limitation_policy: Rows that cannot be measured yet stay planned or in-progress with a concrete blocker instead of a speed claim.

rows.same_scene_policy: Use identical DART-owned candidate fixtures and sort keys on CPU and GPU; compare candidate ids, primitive ids, and conservative activation distances.
rows.tolerance_policy: Candidate-set parity is exact; any floating distance counter must stay within 1e-10 absolute error.
rows.timing_policy: Report setup, host_to_device, kernel, device_to_host, readback, and CPU baseline timing.
rows.speedup_policy: Require >=1.25x over the DART CPU candidate path before recording a go claim.
rows.public_api_policy: No public CUDA or backend exposure; private evidence stays under scripts, benchmarks, and detail/compute owners.
rows.limitation_status: Private point-triangle and edge-edge contact-stencil filter parity packets, brute-force all-pairs point-triangle and edge-edge candidate-mask packets, motion-aware swept-AABB point-triangle and edge-edge candidate-list packets, compact device-sorted sweep-and-prune broad-phase packets, compact runtime sweep-buffer endpoint-distance packets, reduced scene-owned runtime candidate-buffer packets, and reduced scene-owned runtime sweep broad-phase packets exist. They cover reduced primitive fixtures, compact CPU sweep candidate buffers, private device-side swept-AABB sorting/count/scatter, candidate buffers extracted from one DART World-owned deformable surface, and scene-owned sweep rows extracted from that same surface; full runtime scene filtering, GPU World::step contact candidate construction, and the speedup gate remain future work.
rows.notes_or_gap: The packet measured exact squared-distance and accepted-count parity for 65536 point-triangle stencils, 65536 edge-edge stencils, a 65536-pair point-triangle all-pairs mask, a 65536-pair edge-edge all-pairs mask, a 65536-pair swept-AABB point-triangle candidate list, a 65536-pair swept-AABB edge-edge candidate list, a 65536-capacity point-triangle sweep broad-phase row, and a 65536-capacity edge-edge sweep broad-phase row on 2026-06-12. It now also records reduced scene-owned runtime sweep broad-phase rows extracted from one DART World deformable surface: the point-triangle scene sweep covers 1966080 possible pairs over 2560 points and 768 triangles, emits 512 compact candidates, and records speedup=0.07057754068474563x; the edge-edge scene sweep covers 5308416 possible pairs over 2304 surface edges, emits 1536 compact candidates, and records speedup=0.06350297113311432x. Compact runtime buffer rows still consume CPU sweep candidate keys, and reduced scene-owned runtime candidate-buffer rows still cover one DART World deformable surface. The top-level packet speedup was 0.024497841563440446x, so this remains in-progress rather than measured.

rows.same_scene_policy: Use identical conservative CCD fixtures, step directions, and feasibility margins on CPU and GPU.
rows.tolerance_policy: Time-of-impact and maximum feasible step fraction must stay within 1e-6 absolute error for the mixed endpoint-linear, sampled-curved, and scene-owned runtime CCD packet; endpoint-linear primitive rows still record exact step-bound parity.
rows.timing_policy: Report setup, transfer, CCD kernel, line-search kernel, readback, and CPU baseline timing separately.
rows.speedup_policy: Require >=1.25x over the DART CPU CCD/line-search path before recording a go claim.
rows.public_api_policy: No public CUDA or backend exposure; private evidence stays under scripts, benchmarks, and detail/compute owners.
rows.limitation_status: Private endpoint-linear point-triangle and edge-edge CCD line-search parity packets, sampled rigid-curved point-triangle and edge-edge trajectory packets, and reduced scene-owned runtime point-triangle and edge-edge CCD rows exist. The scene point-triangle row now consumes the full 512 runtime candidate set split across 256 static-triangle and 256 moving-triangle candidates. Analytic curved CCD, full scene-level line-search feasibility, and the top-level/runtime speedup gate remain unproven.
rows.notes_or_gap: The packet measured CPU/GPU step-bound parity within tolerance for 65536 endpoint-linear point-triangle pairs, 65536 endpoint-linear edge-edge pairs, 65536 sampled rigid-curved point-triangle trajectories over 524288 segments, 65536 sampled rigid-curved edge-edge trajectories over 524288 segments, 512 runtime point-triangle CCD pairs split across 256 static-triangle and 256 moving-triangle candidates from one DART World deformable surface, and 1536 runtime edge-edge CCD pairs from the same surface on 2026-06-12. The top-level packet covers 264192 pairs and 1181696 sampled/evaluated segments, records hit_count=197120, max_result_abs_error=5.551115123125783e-17, and minimum primitive-family speedup=0.3492306596158999x (meets_speedup_gate=false because the reduced scene-owned runtime point-triangle row is below the speedup gate). The row remains in-progress until analytic curved CCD, full scene-level line-search feasibility, and runtime speedup have evidence.

rows.cpu_evidence_command: pixi run bm --target bm_ipc_barrier_kernel --build-type Release -- --benchmark_report_aggregates_only=true --benchmark_out=.benchmark_results/plan083/gpu/barrier_cpu.json --benchmark_out_format=json && pixi run bm --target bm_ipc_tangent_stencil --build-type Release -- --benchmark_report_aggregates_only=true --benchmark_out=.benchmark_results/plan083/gpu/friction_cpu.json --benchmark_out_format=json
rows.same_scene_policy: Use identical primitive distances, tangent bases, lagged normal forces, and friction displacements on CPU and GPU.
rows.tolerance_policy: Barrier value, gradient, Hessian, tangent basis, and friction work counters must stay within row-specific 1e-10 to 1e-9 tolerances.
rows.timing_policy: Report setup, transfer, barrier kernel, friction kernel, PSD/local projection timing where used, readback, and CPU baseline timing.
rows.speedup_policy: Require >=1.25x over DART CPU local-kernel timing before recording a go claim.
rows.public_api_policy: No public CUDA or backend exposure; private evidence stays under scripts, benchmarks, and detail/compute owners.
rows.limitation_status: Private scalar barrier/friction local-kernel parity, point-triangle primitive barrier-gradient parity, point-triangle, edge-edge, point-edge, and point-point tangent-stencil parity packets, point-triangle/point-point/point-edge/edge-edge primitive barrier-Hessian parity, point-triangle/point-point/point-edge/edge-edge primitive barrier-Hessian PSD-projection parity, and reduced scene-owned point-triangle/point-edge/point-point/edge-edge barrier-Hessian runtime rows exist. Broader sparse Hessian assembly, additional runtime contact rows, and top-level/runtime speedup remain future evidence.
rows.notes_or_gap: The packet measured max_result_abs_error=7.844391802791506e-12 for 65536 scalar, point-triangle gradient, all four primitive-family tangent-stencil sample sets, point-triangle/point-point/point-edge/edge-edge barrier-Hessian samples, point-triangle/point-point/point-edge/edge-edge Hessian PSD-projection samples, and reduced scene-owned point-triangle/point-edge/point-point/edge-edge barrier-Hessian runtime rows on 2026-06-12. The point-triangle runtime row extracts 512 point-triangle candidates from one DART World deformable surface with 2560 nodes and 768 triangles, records 256 active barriers, max_result_abs_error=1.7763568394002505e-15, and speedup=0.17984862275960234x. The point-edge runtime row expands 512 source point-triangle candidates into 1536 point-edge candidates over the same scene surface, records 768 active barriers, max_result_abs_error=8.881784197001252e-16, and speedup=0.49484632935106865x. The point-point runtime row expands the same 512 source point-triangle candidates into 1536 point-point pairs over triangle vertices, records 256 active barriers, max_result_abs_error=0.0, and speedup=0.21761886815815773x. The edge-edge runtime row consumes 1536 scene-owned edge-edge candidates from the same scene surface, records 1280 active barriers, max_result_abs_error=2.220446049250313e-15, and speedup=0.6630850632538113x. The edge-edge primitive barrier-Hessian row records 59578 active barriers, max_result_abs_error=3.623767952376511e-13, and speedup=1.592433352051249x. The edge-edge Hessian PSD-projection row records 59578 active barriers, max_result_abs_error=4.334310688136611e-13, psd_projection_ns=121906741.0, and speedup=2.045170785242439x. The top-level packet speedup was 0.17984862275960234x, so this row remains in-progress rather than measured.

rows.same_scene_policy: Use identical symmetric block batches and solver-realistic PSD dimensions on CPU and GPU.
rows.tolerance_policy: Projected blocks must match the CPU reference within 1e-9 absolute error for double precision rows.
rows.timing_policy: Report setup, host_to_device, PSD kernel, solve=0 for local projection, device_to_host, readback, and CPU reference timing.
rows.speedup_policy: Require >=1.25x over DART CPU PSD projection before recording a PLAN-083 speed claim.
rows.public_api_policy: No public CUDA or backend exposure; existing PSD backend install seam remains private and opt-in.
rows.limitation_status: Measured packet exists for 4096 symmetric 12x12 PSD blocks; this is a local-kernel packet, not a scene-level GPU claim.
rows.notes_or_gap: The PSD projection packet measured max_result_abs_error=2.4868995751603507e-14 and speedup=4.451557656446166x on 2026-06-09; remaining GPU rows stay planned until their own kernels and same-scene packets land.

rows.same_scene_policy: Use identical sparse matrices, right-hand sides, pivots, solver tolerances, and deterministic reduction order on CPU and GPU.
rows.tolerance_policy: Residual norm, step norm, and barrier feasibility must stay within the accepted sparse Newton tolerance for the same scene.
rows.timing_policy: Report setup, transfer, assembly kernel, sparse residual matvec, linear solve, readback, CPU assembly, and CPU solve timing separately.
rows.speedup_policy: Require >=1.25x over the matched DART CPU sparse Newton packet before recording a go claim.
rows.public_api_policy: No public CUDA or backend exposure; any residency or solver owner must stay behind private implementation boundaries.
rows.limitation_status: Private reduced diagonal assembly/solve, reduced scene-owned diagonal assembly/solve, pair-slot off-diagonal sparse-block assembly, reduced scene-owned sparse off-diagonal surface-edge assembly, reduced scene-owned sparse graph construction/assembly, reduced scene-owned nonlinear distance-equality assembly, reduced scene-owned nonlinear distance-equality solve, reduced scene-owned capped nonlinear distance-equality convergence, sparse block residual matvec, reduced scene-owned sparse residual matvec, fixed-iteration sparse Jacobi solve, reduced scene-owned sparse Jacobi solve, capped-iteration sparse CG solve, reduced scene-owned sparse CG solve, and sparse equality-reduced diagonal solve packets exist. They cover deterministic reduced fixtures plus one DART World-owned deformable-surface diagonal row, scene-derived triangle incidence counts, sparse graph rows/blocks, surface-edge distance equality linearization rows/blocks, one deterministic Jacobi-style nonlinear equality correction step, a damped eight-iteration geometric distance residual loop with endpoint residual reporting, sparse residual row, sparse Jacobi row, sparse CG row, and reduced surface-edge sparse off-diagonal pair slots with exact CPU/GPU local-output parity, but production sparse Hessian graph deduplication, full runtime sparse Hessian graph construction and assembly, direct/global sparse factorization, production nonlinear equality convergence policy/solving, GPU World::step assembly/solve integration, and the top-level speedup gate remain future work.
rows.notes_or_gap: The packet measured same-fixture CPU/GPU parity for 65,536 diagonal assembly/solve rows, 65,536 off-diagonal sparse-block rows, an 8,192-block sparse residual matvec, an 8,192-block fixed-iteration sparse Jacobi solve, an 8,192-block capped-iteration sparse CG solve, and a 49,152-entry sparse equality-reduced diagonal solve on 2026-06-13. It also measured reduced scene-owned rows extracted from one DART World deformable surface: 2,560 scene nodes, 768 surface triangles, and 2,304 surface-edge pair slots. The reduced scene-owned sparse graph assembly row counts triangle incidence on GPU, emits 2,560 per-node diagonal rows and 2,304 oriented surface-edge 6x6 sparse blocks, and records 82,944 block entries. The reduced scene-owned nonlinear equality assembly row linearizes one surface-edge distance equality constraint per oriented scene edge, emits two local Newton rows and one 6x6 sparse block per constraint, records 2,304 constraints, 4,608 rows, 2,304 sparse blocks, max_constraint_residual_abs=0.023006792475267046, max_result_abs_error=2.8421709430404007e-13, max_diagonal=1001.9679687500002, max_gradient_abs=1.2260273625132818, max_block_abs=1001.9674687500002, and speedup=0.11643289378198905x. The reduced scene-owned nonlinear equality solve row reuses that surface-edge linearization, applies regularization=0.05, accumulates one deterministic Jacobi-style correction step into 9,215 active generalized coordinates, and reports max_post_solve_linearized_residual_abs=0.07899550902133222, step_norm=0.3288379160439768, max_result_abs_error=2.8421709430404007e-13, and speedup=0.07249443690039502x. The new reduced scene-owned nonlinear equality convergence row uses zero-damping geometric distance constraints, applies eight damped correction iterations, reports converged=false for residual_tolerance=1e-05, reduces max distance residual from 0.023006792475267046 to 0.022842203667180705, and records step_norm=0.28479436450197887, max_result_abs_error=4.547473508864641e-13, and speedup=0.09527533471087142x. Existing scene-owned diagonal assembly/solve, sparse off-diagonal assembly, sparse residual, sparse Jacobi, sparse CG, sparse graph assembly, nonlinear equality assembly, nonlinear equality solve, and nonlinear equality convergence rows remain reduced packet evidence only. The top-level assembly/solve packet records max_result_abs_error=4.547473508864641e-13, residual_norm=3.705385775963348e-13, and speedup=0.012632667834780719x, so this row remains in-progress rather than measured.

rows.roadmap_item: same-scene CPU/GPU result parity and speedup
rows.same_scene_policy: Use the same DART scene id, timestep, material parameters, contact geometry, initial state, and acceptance invariant for CPU and GPU.
rows.tolerance_policy: Use row-specific invariants from cpu-scene-corpus.json; no speed row may pass if the CPU/GPU final state or diagnostics violate those invariants.
rows.timing_policy: Report setup, transfer, candidate/CCD/local-kernel/assembly/solve subphase timings, readback, and full CPU step timing.
rows.speedup_policy: Require speedup over DART CPU at matched accuracy; if the paper source lacks a GPU number, compare only against DART CPU and record that limitation.
rows.public_api_policy: No public CUDA or backend exposure; scene evidence stays private/manual until a separate public promotion gate exists.
rows.limitation_status: Reduced state-batch scene parity packet exists, but it covers DART scene state extraction plus private rigid-body state-batch CUDA rollout only. It does not run a GPU World::step path, contact candidate construction, CCD, barrier/friction assembly, sparse equality reduction, or global Newton solve.
rows.notes_or_gap: The reduced hanging-bridge state-batch packet measured max_result_abs_error=1.1102230246251565e-16 and speedup=18.792052761946252x for 4096 replicated scenes, 7 bodies per scene, and 64 steps on 2026-06-11. Because it bypasses the GPU contact/constraint/Newton solver path, this row remains in-progress rather than measured.
